title: Living in Boulder vlog 12 -  7 -  18: Slept all day. 100 miles a day still. Reefifying

transcript.text: Sup, yon-san, livin' in bold V-LOG.
My yesterday's video didn't make it onto YouTube because it was more than 10 minutes or something.
And I had to verify it, so I hit the verify button, and then I forgot to upload it.
So I'll upload that video, and then I'll upload this little video and see if it works.
But maybe I'll talk for less than 10 minutes just in case I'm not verified or whatever.
It doesn't work.
So, yeah, yesterday I did my number, and I think it was number nine ride in a row.
And I think I did 60 miles, 65 miles and 60 or something like that.
And so I think it was 600-mile days in a row.
So that's like 600-mile week almost, or today would make it.
But, yeah, yesterday I was watching music videos and, yeah, just watching music videos the whole time.
And then I stopped.
I should have eaten a protein drink, but I felt kind of queasy.
And I'm really stoned right now.
I just got really high, and then I remembered, oh, shoot, I need to do my V-log.
I was just going to ride my bike.
And, yeah, so, but I rode 100 miles in 556.
That's the fastest ever that I've done.
And that broke my day the other time before by 20 minutes.
So now, but then yesterday I didn't drink the protein drink.
I slept all day today, but I just had a meal.
Yeah, so I don't know.
I don't know what to expect.
I don't really feel like – one part of my brain is like, oh, you should try to break the record again
And, like, you know, be even more efficient and pedal harder and smoke less and all that.
And then another sarpine bird is like, no, you should chill.
So I just slept all day long.
That's all I did.
I just slept.
I wasn't because I just could, and it's nice to sleep.
But to get up, I could have gotten up and done stuff, but I didn't.
So all I'm doing is riding my bike and sleeping.
And for breakfast, after I rode, I had these chicken, these McNuggets,
And I got at Burger King the other day and some, like, cold French fries that I was kind of nibbling on.
But that's all I had to eat until my – yeah, I went over to my mom's house and had, like, two bowls of soup.
She's been making this, like, shrimp vegetable soup, so.
But, yeah, I'm going to get hungry.
I'm definitely going to need to power eat a whole lot of extra, that special rocket fuel that they make these days, that gel.
So I ordered a tin on Amazon, a tin, 16.9-ounce gels, the banana flavor, those are the $15.
The berries are $25, and the – I think I already talked about this.
I don't know if it's $21, so that's the cheapest.
So I went through most of it yesterday.
Yeah, I think there's just a little bit left.
So the rule of thumb is – and my appetite hasn't even kicked in yet – is a 16.9-ounce gel for a six-hour ride, a 100-mile ride.
Bro.
Okay, so that's all I have to say.
I'm going to go on there and start riding.
You listen to music?
Smoke a lot of weed, right?
You know what I'm saying?
I'm like, yeah, because I think I'm going to quit weed, so I might as well quit weed on a blowout.
You know what I mean?
Just smoke my ass off.
Why the hell not?
Right there on YouTube, laughing my ass off.
All the Nazis can't do a goddamn thing about it.
And then they finally be like, you know what, maybe we should just spend our money and buy bicycles and do bike streaming and do audiobook.
Somebody wanted me to do a video about imperialism versus empiricism – or rationalism.
Imperialism.
Empiricism versus rationalism.
And, yeah, I can't wait to do that one.
But that means I have to listen to some books, and I'm not going to do that until I'm done listening to music.
Yes.
Okay, so anyways, I'm done babbling.
Okay, ciao, ciao.
Ciao.
Ciao.
